completeness.bounded_by: OSRS skills where the main(GP/XP)-vs-ironman(gather) cost split is meaningful (the buyable + gathering skills)
known_missing: Agility, Thieving, Hunter, Slayer, and the Melee combat skills (Attack/Strength/Defence/Hitpoints) are intentionally NOT given paired records: they have no gp_per_xp-vs-gather split (see _excluded).

Mining/Fishing/Woodcutting are included as gather=gather for BOTH families (no buyable-XP route); they are inside the 15-skill universe but their split is marginal (only incidental gear/teleport GE spend), as noted per record.

Per-method gp/xp values are NOT exhaustively rendered: only headline buyable methods carry a snapshot 'value' (Prayer, Construction, Herblore, Crafting, Magic). Others use honest nulls because the wiki computes per-product gp/xp from live GE prices across many sub-methods.
Snapshot gp/xp values are price-volatile (basis recorded per value); they will drift as GE prices change.

pricing_basis/realization_channel for gather records is non-GP: pure-gather records use pricing_basis=none / realization_channel=n/a (cost is gather-time, not coins); only Construction/Crafting/Magic ironman carry store-coin spend (pricing_basis=mixed / realization_channel=shop). Per-record store/coin amounts are NOT priced (left to gather-time), so no GP value is claimed for them.

fix_notes: 2026-06-17: P1 gate fix — ensured every record carries the four canonical account-gate fields (audience, pricing_basis, realization_channel, requires_ge). Added realization_channel (previously absent). Remapped pricing_basis enum: gp_per_xp->ge; gather pure->none; gather with store coins (Construction/Crafting/Magic ironman)->mixed. realization_channel: gp_per_xp->ge; gather pure->n/a; gather-with-store->shop. audience derived from account_family. Originals preserved (cost_basis, realization, value.pricing_basis); legacy pricing_basis="value" kept under pricing_basis_orig. No new fetches; values derived from existing records/source.

2026-06-17: gp/xp SIGN normalization — normalized every snapshot value.gp_per_xp to the OSRS Wiki sign convention (NEGATIVE = cost, positive = profit). The wiki computes the GP/XP column as (product_GE_price - material_cost)/xp and writes costs as negatives (verified in source: Magic Imbue wikitext literally states cost '-{{GEP|Astral rune|2}}/86 round 1' per xp; Crafting glassblowing column = ({{GEPT|item}} - {{GEP|Molten glass}})/xp). Flipped the 4 cost values that were stored unsigned/positive: Prayer (11.77 -> -11.77, dragon bones), Construction (14.55 -> -14.55, mahogany plank), Herblore (6.38 -> -6.38, attack mix), Magic (2.3 -> -2.3, Magic Imbue). Crafting glassblowing value (-1.4) was already correctly signed but was MISLABELED 'slightly profitable' in notes — it is a slight COST (low-level glass items sell for less than the molten glass consumed); corrected the label/notes. No value magnitudes changed except sign; no new fetches. Verified signs against existing raw wikitext (ptp_Magic, ptp_Crafting, ptp_Herblore) and a live OSRS Wiki check of Pay-to-play Crafting training.

records.method: Offer bones (e.g. dragon / superior dragon bones) at a gilded altar in a POH with both incense burners lit (350% xp/bone)

value.basis: gp/xp, GE prices via OSRS Wiki {{GEP}} live templates, snapshot @ 2026-06-17 (price-volatile); wiki sign convention: negative = cost
value.note: Snapshot; wiki recomputes from live GE prices and drifts. Negative = cost per xp (offering bones is a pure cost).

records.notes: Fastest Prayer is buying bones on the GE and burning them at a gilded altar. Wiki snapshot (cost, negative sign): dragon bones ~-11.77 gp/xp, superior dragon bones ~-42.78 gp/xp. Requires 75 Construction (boostable) for the altar. 'Most Prayer training methods are relatively expensive.'

records.method: Gather bones yourself (kill green/blue/red/frost dragons, bosses, Slayer) then offer at Chaos altar / gilded altar / Ectofuntus
records.notes: Wiki: 'Prayer is a slower skill to train for ironmen due to having to obtain bones themselves.' Chaos altar (lvl 38 Wilderness, 700% effective xp/bone w/ no-consume) is most efficient but risky; HCIM advised to use Ectofuntus instead. Dragon bones from Wilderness/Myths' Guild/blue dragons. gp_hr null: cost is gather-time, not coins.

records.method: Build & remove furniture (e.g. mahogany/teak) using GE-bought planks; Mahogany Homes or oak larders at lower levels

value.basis: gp/xp, GE prices via OSRS Wiki {{GEP}} live templates, snapshot @ 2026-06-17 (price-volatile); wiki sign convention: negative = cost
value.note: Snapshot; wiki recomputes from live GE prices and drifts. Negative = cost per xp (planks are consumed for no return).

records.notes: Mains buy planks on the GE. Wiki snapshot gp/xp (cost, negative sign): oak plank ~-7.93, teak plank ~-9.21, mahogany plank ~-14.55. 'Using planks costs money... use the highest-tier planks for the fastest experience rates, or look into cheaper alternatives if trying to save money.'

records.method: Make planks yourself (sawmill / Plank Make spell from gathered logs) then build furniture; Mahogany Homes for plank efficiency
records.notes: Irons cut/convert their own logs into planks (sawmill or Plank Make) rather than buying planks on the GE. Mahogany Homes recommended to stretch limited self-made planks. Cost is woodcutting + plank-making gather-time + sawmill coins (store), not GE plank spend.

records.method: Buy herbs + secondaries on the GE, clean & make potions (e.g. attack/strength/prayer/super potions)

value.basis: gp/xp, GE prices via OSRS Wiki {{GEP}} live templates, snapshot @ 2026-06-17 (price-volatile); wiki sign convention: negative = cost
value.note: Snapshot; wiki recomputes from live GE prices and drifts. Negative = cost per xp (this example method is a net loss; some other potions can be profit-positive).

records.notes: Mains buy grimy herbs and secondaries on the GE. Per-potion gp/xp varies by potion; example attack mix ~-6.38 gp/xp (cost, negative sign; wiki table snapshot). Many high-tier potions cost notably more per xp; a minority of methods can be profit-positive.

records.method: Grow/gather herbs (farming herb runs, farming contracts, Master Farmer pickpocket, Managing Miscellania, Slayer drops) + gather secondaries; or Mastering Mixology
records.notes: Wiki: ingredients come from 'farming, monster drops, and minigames (notably Managing Miscellania).' Farming contracts (45 Farming) are the best herb-seed source; Master Farmer pickpocketing (38 Thieving). Mastering Mixology (60+/81) needs no specific herbs/secondaries. Cost is herb/secondary gather-time, not GE spend.

records.method: Buy seeds + compost on the GE and run tree/herb patches (herb runs, tree runs)
records.notes: Mains buy tree seeds, herb seeds and compost/supercompost on the GE; gp/xp varies enormously by run type and seed prices (tree seeds especially expensive). Wiki gives per-run costs rather than a single gp/xp; left null (honest null).

records.method: Gather seeds yourself (farming contracts, bird nests, Master Farmer pickpocket, mole parts, Tithe Farm) and make own compost
records.notes: Wiki 'Obtaining seeds': farming contracts (best), bird nests, Master Farmer pickpocketing, etc. Irons make their own compost and run patches with self-gathered seeds. Cost is seed/compost gather-time, not GE spend.

records.method: Buy raw food on the GE and cook (e.g. cooked karambwan, jugs of wine, bake pie, cooking fish)
records.notes: Mains buy raw fish / ingredients on the GE; many Cooking methods are cheap or even profitable per xp (selling cooked food). gp/xp varies by food; left null. Karambwan cooking 30-99 is the standard fast route.

records.method: Catch/gather the raw food yourself (Fishing, hunter, farming sweetcorn, karambwans) then cook
records.notes: Wiki iron route: sardines -> trout/salmon -> sweetcorn -> cakes -> karambwans, all self-caught/grown. Cost is fishing/farming gather-time, not GE spend. Burn-rate management matters since raw food is hand-gathered.

records.method: Buy materials on the GE (molten glass, d'hide, battlestaves, gems, gold bars) and craft

value.basis: gp/xp, GE prices via OSRS Wiki {{GEP}} live templates, snapshot @ 2026-06-17 (price-volatile); wiki sign convention: negative = cost
value.note: Snapshot; wiki recomputes from live GE prices and drifts. Negative = cost per xp (glassblowing low-level items are a net loss, not a profit).

records.notes: Mains buy molten glass / dragonhide / battlestaves / gems on the GE. Glassblowing snapshot ~-1.40 gp/xp (a slight cost, not profit: low-level glass items sell for less than the molten glass consumed; wiki: 'Most low-level items are not viable due to high cost'). Battlestaves/d'hide vary; some Crafting methods are genuinely profitable (those would show a positive gp/xp).

records.method: Gather materials yourself: make molten glass (buckets of sand + soda ash, or Superglass Make), gather gold/silver ore, hides from Slayer/PvM, gems from mining
records.notes: Wiki iron route: glassblowing 35-99 buying buckets of sand + soda ash from stores (store, not GE) then Superglass Make 61+, plus self-gathered gold/silver ore and hides. Cost is ore/hide/sand gather-time (+ store coins), not GE material spend.

records.method: Buy bars/ore on the GE and smith (Blast Furnace gold bars, dart tips, cannonballs, Giants' Foundry, 3-bar rune items)
records.notes: Mains buy ore/bars on the GE. Blast Furnace gold bars 40-99 is the standard fast main route (buy gold ore). gp/xp varies by product; some high-level routes (rune items + High Alch) approach break-even. Left null due to per-product variance.

records.method: Mine your own ore, smelt bars (Blast Furnace), then smith; Giants' Foundry, cannonballs, Zalcano
records.notes: Irons mine ore and smelt their own bars before smithing. Iron dart tips 15-30/40, then Gold Ore Blast Furnace 40-99 (self-mined gold). Cost is mining/smelting gather-time, not GE bar spend.

records.method: Buy runes (+ items to alch) on the GE: High Level Alchemy, enchanting bolts, Magic Imbue, splashing

value.basis: gp/xp, GE prices via OSRS Wiki {{GEP}} live templates, snapshot @ 2026-06-17 (price-volatile); wiki sign convention: negative = cost
value.note: Snapshot; wiki recomputes from live GE prices and drifts. Negative = cost per xp; wiki itself writes this as a negative ('costs -{{GEP|Astral rune|2}}/86 round 1' per xp).

records.notes: Mains buy runes (and alch fodder) on the GE. High Level Alchemy is the staple passive route and is often net-profitable per cast (would be a positive gp/xp). Magic Imbue snapshot ~-2.30 gp/xp (cost, negative sign). Enchanting bolts 4-55/99 also buyable.

records.method: Gather/craft own runes (Runecraft, GotR, drops, shop) and alch fodder; combat/utility spells via Slayer & quests
records.notes: Irons supply their own runes (runecrafting, Guardians of the Rift, monster drops, Magic shops - store not GE) and alch fodder from drops. Bursting/barraging 65-99 via Slayer doubles as Magic xp. Cost is rune gather-time (+ store), not GE rune spend.

records.method: Buy ammo on the GE (chinchompas for chinning, cannonballs, bolts/arrows) and train (Chinning, dwarf multicannon, NMZ)
records.notes: Mains buy chinchompas / cannonballs / bolts on the GE. Chinning maniacal/skeletal monkeys 45-99 with bought red/black chinchompas is the fast (expensive) route. gp/xp depends on chin/ammo price; left null.

records.method: Catch own chinchompas (Hunter) and make own ammo; Dorgeshuun crossbow, bosses/monsters, crabs, NMZ
records.notes: Irons catch their own chinchompas via Hunter (box trapping) and fletch/make their own ammo. Chinchompas 55+ self-caught. Cost is hunter/ammo gather-time, not GE chin/ammo spend.

records.method: Buy materials on the GE (logs, bow strings, gems for bolt tips, dart tips) and fletch (bows, broad arrows, darts, tipping bolts)
records.notes: Mains buy logs/bowstrings/gems on the GE. Fletching is frequently profitable per xp for mains (e.g. bolts, bows). gp/xp varies and is often negative (profit); left null.

records.method: Gather materials yourself: cut own logs, spin own bow strings (flax/Crafting), cut own gems for bolt tips; broad arrows via Slayer points
records.notes: Irons cut their own logs, make their own bow strings, and cut their own gems. Broad arrows 52-99 use Slayer-reward broad arrowheads. Cost is log/string/gem gather-time, not GE material spend.

records.method: Buy logs on the GE and burn them (line-burning) or make pyre logs; Wintertodt 50+
records.notes: Mains buy logs on the GE and burn them; pure cost (logs are consumed for no return) so gp/xp is positive but logs are cheap. Wintertodt 50+ avoids buying logs and gives supplies. gp/xp varies by log tier; left null.

records.method: Cut own logs (Woodcutting) and burn them; Wintertodt 50+ (no logs needed) is the staple
records.notes: Irons cut their own logs. Wintertodt 50+ is the recommended iron route since it needs no pre-gathered logs and rewards supplies/loot. Cost is woodcutting gather-time, not GE log spend.

records.method: Mine ore directly (iron ore powermining, 3-tick granite, Motherlode Mine, Volcanic Mine)
records.notes: Mining is a gathering skill: XP is gathered, not bought, for BOTH families. The only main/iron split is incidental GE spend on stamina potions, teleports and pickaxe/gear upgrades; no buyable-XP route exists. cost_basis = gather for both. Included to show no meaningful gp_per_xp route.

records.method: Mine ore directly (iron ore, 3-tick granite, sandstone, Motherlode Mine, Volcanic Mine)
records.notes: Identical gather route to mains. Irons additionally rely on Mining as the upstream supply for Smithing/Crafting. Cost is gather-time.

records.method: Fish directly (fly fishing, Barbarian Fishing, Tempoross, 2-tick swordfish/tuna, minnows)
records.notes: Fishing is a gathering skill: XP gathered, not bought, for BOTH families. Marginal main/iron split is only stamina/teleport/gear GE spend; no buyable-XP route. cost_basis = gather for both.

records.method: Fish directly (fly fishing 20-58, Barbarian Fishing 58-99, Tempoross, karambwans, minnows)
records.notes: Identical gather route to mains; also the upstream supply for Cooking. Cost is gather-time.

records.method: Chop trees directly (regular -> teak; Wintertodt 50-60 for supplies; Forestry events)
records.notes: Woodcutting is a gathering skill: XP gathered, not bought, for BOTH families. Marginal main/iron split is only axe/log-basket/Lumberjack-outfit GE spend; no buyable-XP route. cost_basis = gather for both.

records.method: Chop trees directly (regular up to ~50, Wintertodt 50-60, teak 35-99); upstream supply for Construction/Firemaking/Fletching
records.notes: Identical gather route to mains; also the upstream supply for Firemaking/Fletching/Construction. Cost is gather-time.

records.method: Guardians of the Rift, or craft runes from bought pure/daeyalt essence (lavas, bloods/souls); buy essence on the GE
records.notes: Mains can buy pure essence (and historically daeyalt) on the GE to power runecrafting (lava runes 23-99, blood/soul 77-99). Essence cost per xp is low but nonzero; left null due to method variance. GotR is the standard route for both families.

records.method: Gather essence yourself (mine pure essence, daeyalt) and craft; Guardians of the Rift; Arceuus blood/soul runes
records.notes: Wiki: 'Acquiring essence' is the iron gating step - mine your own pure/daeyalt essence. GotR 10-99 needs no pre-bought essence and is HCIM-friendly. Cost is essence gather-time, not GE essence spend.

_excluded.reason: No meaningful main-vs-iron cost split: Agility is trained the same way (rooftop courses, Hallowed Sepulchre, Brimhaven) for all account families; XP is neither bought (gp_per_xp) nor materially gathered. No GP/XP route exists for mains.

No meaningful main-vs-iron cost split: pickpocketing/stalls (blackjacking, artefacts) is identical for all families; no consumables bought per xp and nothing gathered. Thieving is itself a supply method (coins/seeds) rather than a cost-split skill.

No meaningful main-vs-iron cost split: birdhouses, Hunters' Rumours, chinchompas, herbiboar are trapped the same way for all families; XP is gathered, not bought. (Hunter is the upstream supply for iron Ranged chinchompas, noted under Ranged.)

No meaningful gp_per_xp-vs-gather split: Slayer XP is earned by killing assigned monsters for all families. Mains may spend on supplies/gear but there is no buy-XP route; the iron difference is self-supplied gear/consumables, not a per-xp cost basis.
Combat XP is earned by fighting; no gp_per_xp-vs-gather cost split. Main/iron difference is gear/consumable sourcing, not a per-xp training cost. (NMZ/quests common to both.)
